exercises.title: Generic Pair Class
exercises.description:
Create a generics class with the following specifications.

| File | PairDemo.java |
|---|---|

| Class | Type Params | Fields | Methods |
|--------|------------|-----------|----------|
| Pair\<A, B\> | A, B | first: A, second: B | getFirst(): A, getSecond(): B, swap(): Pair\<B, A\> |

Create a Pair\<String, Integer\> and output the swapped Pair\<Integer, String\>.

※ Following the one-class-per-file principle, create: **Pair.java**, **PairDemo.java**
exercises.expectedOutput:
Original pair: (Java, 17)
Swapped: (17, Java)
String pair: (Hello, World)
exercises.hint: Define first (A type) and second (B type) fields in Pair<A,B>. swap() creates a new instance returning Pair<B,A>.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ Multiple type parameters <A,B>: A generics class that accepts two different types.
■ Type swapping: swap() returns Pair<B,A> for type-safe value swapping.
■ toString(): Customizes the object's string representation. Automatically called by println().
■ Diamond operator <>: new Pair<>(...) infers generics arguments via type inference.

exercises.title: Inventory Management System
exercises.description:
Create an inventory management class with the following specifications.

| File | InventoryDemo.java |
|---|---|

| Class | Data Structure | Description |
|--------|-----------|------|
| Inventory | Map\<String, Integer\> | Product name → stock count |

| Method | Return | Description |
|----------|--------|------|
| addStock(String, int) | void | Stock in |
| removeStock(String, int) | boolean | Stock out (false if insufficient) |
| printInventory() | void | Display inventory list |
| getOutOfStock() | List\<String\> | List of out-of-stock products |

※ Following the one-class-per-file principle, create: **InventoryManager.java**, **Inventory.java**
exercises.expectedOutput:
=== Inventory List ===
Apple: 50 units
Banana: 30 units
Orange: 0 units
Out of stock: [Orange]
exercises.hint: Define addStock() and removeStock() methods to manage stock changes. Search for entries with value ≤ 0 for out-of-stock items.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ LinkedHashMap: A Map preserving insertion order, making inventory display order predictable.
■ getOrDefault(): Returns a default value when the key doesn't exist. Prevents NullPointerException.
■ Math.max(0, v): Prevents stock from going negative by capping the lower bound at 0.
■ ArrayList + loop: Dynamically collects out-of-stock products for list display.

exercises.title: Set Operations: Union, Intersection, Difference
exercises.description:
Create a program that uses Set to find the intersection, union, and difference of two lists.

[Required Knowledge] HashSet, retainAll, addAll, removeAll
exercises.expectedOutput:
List A: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
List B: [3, 4, 5, 6, 7]
Intersection: [3, 4, 5]
Union: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7]
Difference(A-B): [1, 2]
exercises.hint: Use Set's retainAll() for intersection, addAll() for union, removeAll() for difference. Make copies before operations since they modify the original Set.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ TreeSet: A sorted set. Output order is predictable with numbers in ascending order.
■ retainAll(Collection): Retains only elements contained in the specified collection (intersection).
■ addAll(Collection): Adds all elements from the specified collection (union).
■ removeAll(Collection): Removes all elements contained in the specified collection (difference).
■ Importance of copying: Set operations are destructive, so copy with new TreeSet<>(list) before operating.

exercises.title: Generic Search Method
exercises.description:
Create a generic method findAll() that searches for elements matching a condition in a list of any type. Verify it works with both Integer and String lists.

[Required Knowledge] Generic methods, Predicate, List
exercises.expectedOutput:
even: [2, 4, 6, 8, 10]
Words with 5+ characters: [Hello, World]
exercises.hint: Define as <T> List<T> findAll(List<T> list, Predicate<T> condition). Use Predicate.test() for condition checking.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ Generic method <T>: Declares a type parameter at the method level; the type is inferred at call time.
■ Predicate<T>: A functional interface that takes T and returns boolean.
■ Lambda expression: n -> n % 2 == 0 concisely implements a Predicate.
■ Type inference: T is inferred as Integer for findAll(numbers, ...) and String for findAll(words, ...).

exercises.title: LRU Cache Implementation
exercises.description:
Implement an **LRU (Least Recently Used) cache**. When capacity is exceeded, the least recently accessed element is automatically removed.

| Method | Return | Description |
|---------|--------|------|
| get(K key) | V | Gets value (updates access order) |
| put(K key, V value) | void | Adds/updates value |
| size() | int | Current element count |

- Automatically remove oldest element when capacity is exceeded
- Both get and put update access order
exercises.expectedOutput:
put(1, A), put(2, B), put(3, C)
get(1) = A
put(4, D) -> Oldest entry 2 is removed
get(2) = null
cache: {1=A, 3=C, 4=D}
exercises.hint: Use LinkedHashMap's accessOrder=true constructor, or implement with HashMap + doubly-linked list.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ LinkedHashMap(capacity, loadFactor, accessOrder)
accessOrder=true moves accessed elements to the end on get or put.

■ removeEldestEntry()
Override this LinkedHashMap hook method to auto-remove the oldest element when capacity is exceeded.

■ Generic class inheritance
LRUCache<K, V> extends LinkedHashMap<K, V> for a general-purpose cache.

exercises.title: Immutable Collections and Advanced Comparator
exercises.description:
Create a program that sorts employee data by **compound conditions** and manages it as **immutable collections**.

| File | Role |
|-------------|------|
| Employee.java | record (name, department, salary, joinYear) |
| EmployeeAnalyzer.java | Main method + analysis logic |

- Group by department
- Sort by salary descending → join year ascending → name ascending
- Return immutable lists with Collections.unmodifiableList
- Output sort results and average salary by department

※ Following the one-class-per-file principle, create: **Employee.java**, **EmployeeAnalyzer.java**
exercises.expectedOutput:
=== Sort Results (Salary Desc → Join Year Asc → Name Asc) ===
Tanaka (Development, 6M, 2018)
Suzuki (Development, 5M, 2019)
Takahashi (Sales, 5.5M, 2017)
Sato (Sales, 4.5M, 2020)

=== Average Salary by Department ===
Sales: 500.0 (10K yen)
Development: 550.0 (10K yen)
exercises.hint: Chain Comparator.comparing().thenComparing() and use reversed() for descending order.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ Compound Comparator
Chains Comparator.comparingInt().reversed().thenComparing() for multiple sort conditions.

■ collectingAndThen
Performs two-step processing (collect then convert to immutable list) in a single collect().

■ TreeMap as Supplier
Passing TreeMap::new as the second argument to groupingBy() sorts keys in natural order.

chapters.title: Functional & Stream Comprehensive Exercises
chapters.subtitle: Comprehensive Exercises Covering Lambda, Functional Interfaces & Stream

exercises.title: Sales Aggregation with Stream
exercises.description:
Create a program using Stream to calculate total sales, the highest-priced product, and the number of products costing 1000 yen or more from a list of SaleRecords (product name and amount).

[Required Knowledge] Stream, filter, map, reduce, max

※ Following the one-class-per-file principle, create: **SaleRecord.java**, **SalesReport.java**
exercises.expectedOutput:
Total: 6800 yen
Highest: Coffee Maker (3500 yen)
Products 1000 yen or more: 3
exercises.hint: Use stream().mapToInt(SaleRecord::price).sum() for totals and max() for the maximum value.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ record: A data class since Java 16. Fields, constructor, and getters are auto-generated.
■ mapToInt(): Intermediate operation converting Stream<T> to IntStream.
■ sum(): Terminal operation that calculates the sum of an IntStream.
■ max() + Comparator: Retrieves the maximum element using a comparison function. Returns Optional.
■ filter().count(): Counts elements matching the condition.

exercises.title: Function Composition Pipeline
exercises.description:
Create a program that composes multiple Function<String,String> transformations (trim, uppercase, add prefix) using andThen() and applies them to a string list.

[Required Knowledge] Function, andThen, Stream, map
exercises.expectedOutput:
[RESULT] HELLO
[RESULT] WORLD
[RESULT] JAVA
exercises.hint: Chain transformations with Function.andThen() and batch-apply with stream().map(composedFunction).
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ Function<T,R>: A functional interface that takes T and returns R.
■ andThen(): A composition method that chains functions, applying left to right.
■ Method reference (String::trim): A concise notation equivalent to lambda s -> s.trim().
■ stream().map().forEach(): Chains transformation and output in a pipeline.

exercises.title: Department Grouping with Stream
exercises.description:
Create a program that groups an employee list (name, department, age) using Stream API and aggregates the count and average age by department.

[Required Knowledge] Collectors.groupingBy, Stream, Map

※ Following the one-class-per-file principle, create: **Employee2.java**, **DepartmentReport.java**
exercises.expectedOutput:
=== Department Summary ===
Sales: 2 members (Average age: 32.5)
Development: 2 members (Average age: 30.0)
HR: 1 members (Average age: 40.0)
exercises.hint: Group by department with Collectors.groupingBy(), then calculate count() and average() from each group's list.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ Collectors.groupingBy(): Groups Stream elements by a specified key into a Map.
■ LinkedHashMap::new: Specifies the Map implementation as second argument to preserve insertion order.
■ record: Concisely defines Employee2 data.
■ mapToInt().average(): Calculates the average of a numeric stream. Returns Optional.
■ printf + %n: Outputs a platform-dependent newline.

exercises.title: Custom Collectors Implementation
exercises.description:
Create a program that uses Stream's collect() to aggregate statistics (sum, average, max, min, count) from an integer list into Map<String,Number>.

[Required Knowledge] Stream, Collectors, IntSummaryStatistics
exercises.expectedOutput:
=== Statistics ===
Count: 6
Total: 321
Average : 53.5
Max: 95
Min: 12
exercises.hint: IntSummaryStatistics lets you get sum, average, max, min, and count all at once.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ IntSummaryStatistics: A class that manages sum, average, max, min, and count together.
■ summaryStatistics(): A terminal operation that calculates statistics from IntStream at once.
■ getCount/getSum/getAverage/getMax/getMin: Methods for retrieving each statistic.
■ mapToInt(Integer::intValue): Converts Stream<Integer> → IntStream.

exercises.title: Data Transformation Pipeline
exercises.description:
Create a program that parses CSV-format string list and uses Stream API for: "name,age,city" → filter by condition → sort by age descending → formatted output.

[Required Knowledge] Stream, map, filter, sorted, Comparator, split

※ Following the one-class-per-file principle, create: **Person.java**, **CsvPipeline.java**
exercises.expectedOutput:
=== People 30+ (Age Descending) ===
Sato (45) - Osaka
Suzuki (38) - Nagoya
Tanaka (32) - Tokyo
exercises.hint: Split CSV with split(","), convert to record Person, and process with Stream. Use Comparator.comparingInt() for age sorting.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ map(): Intermediate operation converting CSV strings to Person records.
■ filter(): Filters for age 30 and above.
■ sorted() + reversed(): Creates an ascending Comparator then reverses for descending.
■ forEach(): Terminal operation that outputs each element.
■ Stream pipeline: Declaratively describes the processing chain of map→filter→sorted→forEach.

exercises.title: String Encryption with Functional Pipeline
exercises.description:
Build a string encryption/decryption pipeline using **Function composition (compose / andThen)**.

| Transform Step | Process |
|-------------|------|
| Step 1 | Add +3 to each character's ASCII code (Caesar cipher) |
| Step 2 | Reverse the string |
| Step 3 | Base64 encode |

- Build encryption pipeline (Step1 → Step2 → Step3) with Function composition
- Also build decryption pipeline (reverse Step3 → reverse Step2 → reverse Step1)
- Verify that encrypting then decrypting returns the original string
exercises.expectedOutput:
Original: Hello
Encrypted: b2ors
Decrypted: Hello
Verification: Match
exercises.hint: Chain Functions with andThen(). Prepare inverse functions for each step.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ Function.andThen()
Chains functions sequentially. f.andThen(g) is equivalent to g(f(x)).

■ Reversible transformations
Prepares inverse functions for each step to build the decryption pipeline. Caesar cipher inverse of +3 is -3.

■ Method reference vs lambda
Static methods returning Functions create a factory pattern for flexible pipeline assembly.

exercises.title: Custom Stream Collector
exercises.description:
Implement the following custom Collectors using **Collector.of()**.

| Collector | Description |
|-----------|------|
| toStats() | Collects sum, average, max, min, count into a Statistics object |
| topN(n) | Collects top N items in descending order as a List |

- Apply to Integer streams
- toStats() implements all of Collector.of()'s supplier / accumulator / combiner / finisher
- topN() uses PriorityQueue for efficient top-N collection
exercises.expectedOutput:
=== Statistics ===
Count: 8
Total: 360
Average : 45.0
Min: 10
Max: 90

=== Top 3 ===
[90, 80, 70]
exercises.hint: Build with Collector.of(() -> new Stats(), (stats, val) -> stats.accept(val), (s1, s2) -> s1.combine(s2), stats -> stats.build()).
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ Collector.of()'s four components
Specify supplier (initialization), accumulator (accumulation), combiner (parallel combining), finisher (final transform).

■ PriorityQueue (min-heap)
topN() uses a min-heap, keeping at most N elements. The smallest is removed first, leaving the top N.

■ combiner
Implements logic for combining two intermediate results for parallel Stream support.

chapters.title: DB & Web Development Comprehensive Exercises
chapters.subtitle: Comprehensive Exercises Covering SQL, JDBC & Spring Boot

exercises.title: Employee Table Design and CRUD
exercises.description:
Design an employee table (id, name, department, salary) and write a program with INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE SQL statements.

[Required Knowledge] CREATE TABLE, INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE, WHERE
exercises.expectedOutput:
CREATE TABLE employees ...
INSERT success: 3 rows
SELECT result: Yamada Taro(Sales)
UPDATE success: Salary updated
DELETE success: 1 row deleted
exercises.hint: Set a PRIMARY KEY in CREATE TABLE and write each CRUD operation in order.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ CREATE TABLE: A DDL statement defining columns, types, and constraints for a table.
■ PRIMARY KEY: A constraint that uniquely identifies each row.
■ INSERT INTO: A DML statement for adding data. Values are specified in the VALUES clause.
■ SELECT ... WHERE: A DML statement for querying data matching conditions.
■ UPDATE ... SET ... WHERE: A DML statement for updating data matching conditions.
■ DELETE FROM ... WHERE: A DML statement for deleting data matching conditions.

exercises.title: JDBC Connection Pattern
exercises.description:
Create a program skeleton for connecting to a database with JDBC and executing parameterized queries with PreparedStatement. Manage resources safely with try-with-resources.

[Required Knowledge] DriverManager, Connection, PreparedStatement, ResultSet, try-with-resources
exercises.expectedOutput:
Connection URL: jdbc:h2:mem:testdb
Schema creation complete
Search results:
  ID=1, Name=ProPro, Price=980
exercises.hint: Connect with DriverManager.getConnection() and bind values to PreparedStatement's ? placeholders with setXxx().
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ DriverManager.getConnection(): Establishes a DB connection by specifying a URL.
■ Connection: An interface representing a database connection. Implements AutoCloseable.
■ PreparedStatement: Safely binds parameters with ? placeholders (prevents SQL injection).
■ setInt(position, value): Binds a value to the ? at the specified position (1-based).
■ ResultSet: Reads SELECT results row by row. Use next() to advance to the next row.
■ try-with-resources: Auto-closes Connection, Statement, and ResultSet.

exercises.title: REST API Design
exercises.description:
Create a Spring Boot REST controller with the following specifications.

| File | ProductController.java |
|---|---|

| Endpoint | HTTP Method | Path | Description |
|--------------|------------|------|----- |
| Get all | GET | /api/products | Returns product list |
| Get one | GET | /api/products/{id} | Searches by ID |
| Create | POST | /api/products | Registers new product |
| Delete | DELETE | /api/products/{id} | Deletes product |
exercises.expectedOutput:
GET /api/products → Product list
GET /api/products/1 → Product detail
POST /api/products → Add product
DELETE /api/products/1 → Delete product
exercises.hint: Use the annotation corresponding to each HTTP method. Receive path parameters with @PathVariable.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ @RestController: Annotation for REST API controllers. Includes @ResponseBody.
■ @RequestMapping: Specifies the base URL path. Combines with method-level mappings.
■ @GetMapping / @PostMapping / @DeleteMapping: Defines handlers for each HTTP method.
■ @PathVariable: Binds the {id} part of the URL path to a method argument.
■ @RequestBody: Converts the HTTP request body JSON to an object.

exercises.title: Transaction Management
exercises.description:
Implement bank transfer processing with JDBC transactions. Rollback on insufficient source balance, commit on success.

[Required Knowledge] Connection.setAutoCommit, commit, rollback, PreparedStatement
exercises.expectedOutput:
Before transfer: A=10000 yen, B=5000 yen
Transfer: A→B 3000 yen
After transfer: A=7000 yen, B=8000 yen
---
Transfer: A→B 20000 yen
Error: Insufficient balance → Rollback
Balance unchanged: A=7000 yen, B=8000 yen
exercises.hint: Start a transaction with conn.setAutoCommit(false), call commit() on success and rollback() on failure.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ setAutoCommit(false): Disables auto-commit, switching to manual transaction management.
■ commit(): Applies all changes within the transaction to the database.
■ rollback(): Rolls back all changes within the transaction to the state before the transaction started.
■ try-catch-finally: A pattern for rollback on exception, commit on success, and restore autoCommit at the end.

exercises.title: API Response Design
exercises.description:
Create a unified format class for API responses.

| File | ApiResponseDemo.java |
|---|---|

| Class | Type Param | Fields | Description |
|--------|------------|-----------|------|
| ApiResponse\<T\> | T | status: int, message: String, data: T | Unified response |

| Factory Method | Return | Description |
|-------------------|--------|------|
| success(T data) | ApiResponse\<T\> | Success response (status=200) |
| error(String msg) | ApiResponse\<T\> | Error response (status=500) |

※ Following the one-class-per-file principle, create: **ApiResponse.java**, **Product.java**, **ApiResponseDemo.java**
exercises.expectedOutput:
Success: {status:200, message:"OK", data:{id:1, name:"Intro to Java"}}
Error: {status:404, message:"Product not found", data:null}
exercises.hint: Define status, message, data fields in ApiResponse<T> and provide success() and error() factory methods.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ Generic class <T>: Allows the response's data field to be any type.
■ Factory methods: Unifies instance creation with success()/error(), making the constructor private.
■ record: Concise Product data definition (Java 16+). Auto-generates constructor, getters, equals, hashCode.
■ null usage: Distinguishes success/error type-safely with data=null for errors.

exercises.title: DAO Pattern Design
exercises.description:
Design a data access layer using the **DAO (Data Access Object) pattern**. Make in-memory and JDBC implementations switchable.

| File | Role |
|-------------|------|
| User.java | User entity (record) |
| UserDao.java | CRUD interface |
| InMemoryUserDao.java | In-memory implementation using Map |
| DaoDemo.java | Main method |

- Define findById / findAll / save / delete in UserDao
- Use Optional for safe access to non-existent IDs

※ Following the one-class-per-file principle, create: **User.java**, **UserDao.java**, **InMemoryUserDao.java**, **DaoDemo.java**
exercises.expectedOutput:
Saved: User[id=1, name=Tanaka, email=tanaka@example.com]
Saved: User[id=2, name=Suzuki, email=suzuki@example.com]
All records: 2 items
ID=1: Optional[User[id=1, name=Tanaka, email=tanaka@example.com]]
ID=99: Optional.empty
After deletion: 1 items
exercises.hint: Define CRUD methods in the UserDao interface and implement with Map<Integer, User> in InMemoryUserDao.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ DAO pattern
Abstracts data access logic with an interface, enabling switchable implementations (in-memory/JDBC/file).

■ Optional
findById() returns Optional, enabling safe access without null checks.

■ List.copyOf()
Defensive copy that prevents external modification.

chapters.title: Concurrency & I/O Comprehensive Exercises
chapters.subtitle: Comprehensive Exercises Covering Threads, File Operations & NIO

exercises.title: Countdown Timer
exercises.description:
Create a 10-second countdown timer displayed on the console using Thread.sleep(). Run the countdown in a separate thread.

[Required Knowledge] Thread, Runnable, sleep, InterruptedException
exercises.hint: Implement the countdown logic with Runnable and use Thread.sleep(1000) to wait 1 second.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ Runnable: A functional interface with run(). Can be concisely implemented with lambda.
■ new Thread(runnable): Creates a thread by passing a Runnable.
■ start(): Starts the thread. Calling run() directly executes in the same thread.
■ Thread.sleep(1000): Pauses the current thread for 1000 milliseconds (1 second).
■ join(): Waits for thread completion. Main thread waits for timer to finish.

exercises.title: File Read and Write
exercises.description:
Create a program that writes to and reads from a text file using java.nio.file.Files. Display content with line numbers.

[Required Knowledge] Files.write, Files.readAllLines, Path, StandardCharsets
exercises.expectedOutput:
File write complete: output.txt
=== File Contents ===
1: Hello, Java!
2: File operations with NIO2
3: Easy and safe
exercises.hint: Write List<String> with Files.write() and read all lines with Files.readAllLines().
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ Path.of(): Static method for creating file paths (Java 11+).
■ Files.write(): Writes data (List<String>) to a Path. Character encoding can be specified.
■ Files.readAllLines(): Reads all file lines as List<String>.
■ StandardCharsets.UTF_8: Character encoding constant. Handles multibyte characters correctly.
■ Files.deleteIfExists(): Deletes a file if it exists.

exercises.title: Multi-Thread Counter
exercises.description:
Create a program that safely increments a counter from multiple threads. Show the difference between using and not using synchronized.

[Required Knowledge] Thread, synchronized, AtomicInteger
exercises.expectedOutput:
=== Without synchronized ===
Expected: 10000, Actual: (varies)
=== With synchronized ===
Expected: 10000, Actual: 10000
=== AtomicInteger ===
Expected: 10000, Actual: 10000
exercises.hint: 10 threads each increment 1000 times. Use synchronized methods or AtomicInteger.incrementAndGet() for thread safety.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ synchronized: When applied to a method, allows exclusive access by one thread at a time (mutual exclusion).
■ AtomicInteger: A class providing lock-free thread-safe integer operations.
■ incrementAndGet(): Atomically increments the value by 1 and returns the new value.
■ Thread.join(): Waits for thread completion, synchronizing the timing for checking results.
■ Race condition: Without synchronized, multiple threads updating simultaneously can lose values.

exercises.title: Directory Explorer
exercises.description:
Create a program that lists files in a specified directory, groups them by extension, and displays with size information.

[Required Knowledge] Files.walk, Stream, Path, BasicFileAttributes, Collectors.groupingBy
exercises.expectedOutput:
=== Directory Explorer: ./src ===
.java files: 5 (Total: 12.5KB)
.txt files: 2 (Total: 1.2KB)
exercises.hint: Recursively explore directories with Files.walk() and group by extension with stream's groupingBy. Get sizes with Files.size().
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ Files.walk(): Recursively explores directories and returns Stream<Path>. Use with try-with-resources.
■ Files::isRegularFile: A method reference filtering regular files (not directories).
■ Collectors.groupingBy(): Groups files by extension as the key.
■ Files.size(): Returns the file's byte count.
■ Stream + Map + functional: Declaratively processes exploration, classification, and aggregation in a Stream pipeline.

exercises.title: Parallel Tasks with ExecutorService
exercises.description:
Create a program that executes multiple tasks in parallel using ExecutorService and receives results via Future<T>. Each task simulates different processing times.

[Required Knowledge] ExecutorService, Callable, Future, newFixedThreadPool
exercises.expectedOutput:
Tasks submitted: 3
Result 1: Calculation done (value: 100)
Result 2: Calculation done (value: 200)
Result 3: Calculation done (value: 300)
All tasks completed
exercises.hint: Create a thread pool with Executors.newFixedThreadPool(), submit Callable<T> tasks, and manage with a list of Future<T>.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ Executors.newFixedThreadPool(n): Creates a thread pool with n threads.
■ Callable<T>: A task that returns a value. Unlike Runnable, can return and throw exceptions.
■ submit(Callable): Submits a task to the thread pool and returns a Future<T>.
■ Future.get(): A blocking method that waits for task completion and retrieves the result.
■ executor.shutdown(): Stops accepting new tasks and waits for running tasks to complete.

exercises.title: Producer-Consumer Pattern
exercises.description:
Implement the **Producer-Consumer** pattern using BlockingQueue.

| File | Role |
|-------------|------|
| Task.java | Task data (record) |
| Producer.java | Thread that puts tasks on the queue |
| Consumer.java | Thread that takes and processes tasks |
| ProducerConsumerDemo.java | Main method |

- Producer generates 5 tasks then puts a stop signal (POISON PILL)
- Consumer stops upon receiving the stop signal
- Queue maximum capacity is 3

※ Following the one-class-per-file principle, create: **Task.java**, **Producer.java**, **Consumer.java**, **ProducerConsumerDemo.java**
exercises.expectedOutput:
[Producer] Task submitted: Task[id=1, name=Task 1]
[Consumer] Processing: Task[id=1, name=Task 1]
[Producer] Task submitted: Task[id=2, name=Task 2]
...
[Producer] All tasks submitted
[Consumer] Stop signal received. Shutting down.
exercises.hint: Share ArrayBlockingQueue<Task>(3) between Producer and Consumer. Use a special Task instance (POISON_PILL) as the stop signal.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ BlockingQueue
put() blocks when the queue is full; take() blocks when empty. Automatically synchronizes between threads.

■ POISON PILL pattern
Puts a special object (POISON_PILL) in the queue to notify the Consumer to stop.

■ record's static field
Defines Task.POISON_PILL as a static field in the record, representing the stop signal with a single instance.

exercises.title: Async Pipeline with CompletableFuture
exercises.description:
Build an order processing pipeline chaining multiple asynchronous operations using **CompletableFuture**.

| Processing Step | Duration | Description |
|-------------|---------|------|
| Stock check | 300ms | Check product inventory |
| Price calculation | 200ms | Calculate discounted price |
| Payment processing | 500ms | Process payment |

- Chain each step with CompletableFuture.supplyAsync() / thenApplyAsync()
- Use exceptionally() for fallback values on errors
- Process 3 products in parallel, wait with allOf()
exercises.expectedOutput:
[Stock Check] Product A: OK
[Price Calc] Product A: 850 yen (15% OFF)
[Payment Done] Product A: 850 yen
[Stock Check] Product B: OK
[Price Calc] Product B: 1700 yen (15% OFF)
[Payment Done] Product B: 1700 yen
[Stock Check] Product C: Out of stock
[Error] Product C: Out of stock
All order processing complete
exercises.hint: Pass results to the next step with thenApplyAsync() and handle errors with exceptionally(). Wait for all with CompletableFuture.allOf().
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ CompletableFuture chain
Builds a 3-stage pipeline with supplyAsync → thenApplyAsync → thenApplyAsync.

■ exceptionally()
Defines fallback processing when an exception occurs mid-pipeline.

■ CompletableFuture.allOf()
Waits for multiple CompletableFutures to complete in parallel. join() blocks until done.

chapters.title: Module & i18n Comprehensive Exercises
chapters.subtitle: Comprehensive Exercises on Module System & Localization

exercises.title: Module Dependency Design
exercises.description:
Design the dependencies of 3 modules (app, service, model) and write the content of each module-info.java.

[Required Knowledge] module declaration, requires, exports
exercises.expectedOutput:
=== Module Structure ===
model: exports com.example.model
service: requires model, exports com.example.service
app: requires service (transitive → model also available)
exercises.hint: Design dependencies as model → service → app. Using requires transitive allows app to also reference model.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ module declaration: Defines a module with module moduleName { }.
■ exports: Makes a package available to other modules.
■ requires: Declares dependency on another module.
■ requires transitive: Transitive dependency. When service transitively requires model, app can also use model.
■ Text blocks: Uses """...""" for readable multiline strings.

exercises.title: Multilingual Greeting Program
exercises.description:
Create a program that switches greeting text in Japanese, English, and French based on locale using Locale and ResourceBundle (Map alternative is OK).

[Required Knowledge] Locale, ResourceBundle (concept), Map, switch
exercises.expectedOutput:
Japanese: Hello, World!
English: Hello, World!
Français: Bonjour, le monde!
exercises.hint: Manage per-locale messages with Map<Locale, Map<String, String>> and retrieve with getOrDefault().
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ Locale: A class representing language, country, and region. Has constants like Locale.JAPANESE, Locale.ENGLISH.
■ getLanguage(): Returns the locale's language code ("ja", "en", etc.).
■ Map.of(): Factory method for creating immutable Maps (Java 9+).
■ getOrDefault(): Returns a default value when the key doesn't exist. Used for unsupported locale fallback.
■ ResourceBundle concept: In real apps, language-specific resources are managed in properties files.

exercises.title: Currency Formatter
exercises.description:
Create a program that displays the same amount in three currency formats (Japanese yen, US dollar, Euro) using NumberFormat.

[Required Knowledge] NumberFormat, Locale, getCurrencyInstance, format
exercises.hint: Get locale-specific formatters with NumberFormat.getCurrencyInstance(Locale.country).
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ NumberFormat.getCurrencyInstance(Locale): Gets a currency formatter appropriate for the locale.
■ format(double): Converts a number to currency-formatted string. Currency symbol and digit separators are automatically applied.
■ Locale.JAPAN: Japanese locale (currency: ¥, no decimals).
■ Locale.US: US locale (currency: $, 2 decimal places).
■ Locale.GERMANY: German locale (currency: €, comma and dot are reversed vs Japan).

exercises.title: Internationalized DateTime Display
exercises.description:
Create a program that displays the same datetime in different formats across multiple locales (Japan, US, France). Consider timezone differences.

[Required Knowledge] ZonedDateTime, DateTimeFormatter, Locale, ZoneId
exercises.expectedOutput:
=== DateTime Internationalization ===
Japan: 2026/1/15 Thursday 09:30 JST
US: Thursday, January 15, 2026 at 7:30 PM CST
France: jeudi 15 janvier 2026 à 01:30 CET
exercises.hint: Create timezone-aware datetime with ZonedDateTime.of() + ZoneId and convert to other timezones with withZoneSameInstant().
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ ZonedDateTime: A datetime class with timezone. Essential for internationalized apps.
■ ZoneId.of(): Specifies a timezone ("Asia/Tokyo", "America/Chicago", etc.).
■ withZoneSameInstant(): Expresses the same instant in a different timezone.
■ DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern(): Defines formatting with a pattern string and locale.
■ Locale argument: Outputs day names (EEEE) and month names (MMM) in the locale's language.

exercises.title: Multilingual Error Messages
exercises.description:
Design a system that returns multilingual error messages based on error codes. Support Japanese and English, returning a default message for unsupported error codes.

[Required Knowledge] Locale, Map, enum, Optional

※ Following the one-class-per-file principle, create: **ErrorCode.java**, **ErrorMessageSystem.java**
exercises.expectedOutput:
=== Japanese ===
E001: Authentication failed
E002: Access denied
E999: An unknown error occurred
=== English ===
E001: Authentication failed
E002: Access denied
E999: An unknown error occurred
exercises.hint: Define error codes with enum and map with Map<Locale, Map<ErrorCode, String>> for multilingual messages.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ enum ErrorCode: An enum that defines error codes in a type-safe manner.
■ ErrorCode.values(): Returns all enum constants as an array.
■ Map<String, Map<ErrorCode, String>>: A two-level Map (language → code → message) for i18n.
■ getOrDefault(): Used for fallback to English for unsupported languages.
■ Locale.getLanguage(): Gets language codes like "ja", "en" for use as Map keys.

exercises.title: Configuration Management with ResourceBundle
exercises.description:
Create a program that manages multilingual configuration messages using **ResourceBundle**.

Switch between Japanese and English messages at runtime.

| Locale | Filename | Greeting | Error Message |
|---------|-----------|------|----------------|
| ja_JP | messages_ja_JP.properties | Welcome | Invalid input |
| en_US | messages_en_US.properties | Welcome | Invalid input |

- Catch MissingResourceException for non-existent keys and return a default value
- Simulate property files in code
exercises.expectedOutput:
=== Japanese ===
greeting = Welcome
error.invalid = Invalid input

=== English ===
greeting = Welcome
error.invalid = Invalid input

=== Non-existent Key ===
unknown.key = (Default value)
exercises.hint: Inherit ListResourceBundle to define resources in program without .properties files.
exercises.solutionExplanation:
■ ListResourceBundle
Defines resources in a Java class instead of property files. Returns a 2D array from getContents().

■ ResourceBundle.getBundle()
Specifies a base name and Locale to find classes by naming convention like MessageBundle_ja_JP.class.

■ MissingResourceException
Thrown when accessing a non-existent key. Catching and returning a default value is a common real-world pattern.
